prompt: Explain the unfamiliar repository architecture and cite the modules that define each major boundary.
Trace an incoming API request across routing, validation, business logic, persistence, and response serialization.
Locate where idempotency is implemented when no relevant filename or symbol is known, and return evidence.
Compare how the three storage adapters implement retries, timeouts, and error normalization across the repository.
Identify cross-cutting security risks in authentication and authorization, with focused file evidence.
Trace how a domain event is produced, transformed, published, consumed, and retried across modules.
Explain how configuration values flow from environment loading through validation into runtime consumers.
CodeGraph identified the billing entry points; now explain every subsystem involved in reconciliation and how responsibilities flow across them.
CodeGraph found the transport error type; trace the repository-wide classification and recovery policy across transport, domain, and persistence modules.
Determine the full change surface for adding a new authentication provider before any implementation begins.
Read src/config.ts and summarize the resolveConfig function already identified there.
Explain the generateInstructions symbol in src/commands/instructions.ts.
Find the exact string CURSOR_COWORKER_TIMEOUT_MS in the repository.
Check whether the repository contains a file named SECURITY.md.
Use the complete CodeGraph result already provided above to list these callers without exploring or synthesizing other modules.
Modify README.md to add one sentence and then run the documentation tests.
Run the existing test/cli.test.ts test file and report whether it passes.
Research the current weather in Paris and summarize the forecast.
CodeGraph already returned the generateInstructions symbol and its callers; summarize that known symbol only.
The previous Cursor Coworker delegation failed; retry the same delegation automatically.
